steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The main statement “they exhibit striking differences in behavior” transitions directly to elaborating examples that illustrate those differences. This is a statement-to-elaboration structure, which requires punctuation that clearly marks the boundary.

steps.title: Match punctuation to the relationship
steps.content:
Option A uses a colon to introduce the elaboration, with “while” setting up a contrast between the two objects (39P/Oterma is active; 83982 Crantor is dormant).

A colon is the standard punctuation for marking a transition from statement to explanation.

Options B and D both contain an unresolved comma splice in the second part (two independent clauses about the two objects connected only by a comma), and Option C uses a comma before “while,” which is too weak for signaling such a major boundary.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
